text.title: SSH Hardening Procedure
text.summary: Step-by-step procedure to apply SSH Hardening end-to-end. Each step names its input, action, output, and (where relevant) a decision-gate.

step.name: snapshot-existing
step.action: Diff against the Modern baseline; identify deltas.

step.name: open-second-terminal
step.action: Keep current SSH session active; open a second SSH session ready to reconnect.
step.decision-gate: If second session cannot be established now, abort — don't reload sshd.

step.name: install-and-reload
step.action: Install to /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/99-hardening.conf, run `sshd -t`, then `systemctl reload ssh`.

step.name: verify-and-record
step.action: Open a THIRD terminal; SSH in fresh. If it works, set second_terminal_verified=true and emit SshPlan.
